channel.title: TreevNFred – Hiking
channel.description: Recent content in Hiking on TreevNFred

item.title: Hiking Ranking Criteria
item.author: sheridad33@gmail.com (Trevor)
item.description:
Overview

These are the general rules I will be using when classifying a hikes difficulty, solitude & beauty

Difficulty

Criteria for a hike to be marked by Trevor as a certain difficulty (may change based on fitness)

  • Goal with the ranking is to keep the average of my ratings around a 5

10/10 

Hikes with a full ten means a hospital trip is needed, legitimately. This difficulty will hopefully never be reached as I would die. I envision this as a trail straight up the side of a mountain >30% grade with little to no trail & with enough miles or elevation to warrant very hard

9/10 

A hike of this difficulty would be something that would scar a man, keeping him up at night just thinking about how ass this hike was. Probably unable to walk for days after 4-5 days. Envisioned as straight up a mountain with maybe one little switchback, and some class 3-4 scrampling perhaps

8/10 

A hike like this is something one may attempt once a summer if crazy. This will have you sore for 2-3 days.

7/10 

This is a classic saturday ass kicker, your out there hiking all day and come back tired BUT can do your thing fairly normally the next day and the hike is nothing to go to therapy over

6/10 

A more advanced than normal hike, wherein you would want to have some hiking and/or fitness before to not get very tired

5/10 

Easy top medium trail condition with some small obsticals (rocks etc) and steep

4/10 

Easy trail with a good incline and spots with steeper incline

3/10 

Easy trail with a consistant incline

2/10 

Easy trail with some incline but not a lot

1/10 

A mostly flat stroll, that most will just do on a saturday

Solitude

This will take into consideration

  • Number of people seen on trail
  • Trail condition if there is one
  • Getting to the hike (trailhead, dirt road etc.)
  • My personal opinion on how isolated it feels

Beauty

This is really just my opinion, but will take into consideration cool finds on the trail as well as the beauty duh.

Wrap Up

item.title: Utah Mountain Range High Points
item.author: sheridad33@gmail.com (Trevor)
item.description:
Overview

This is a long term goal to hike the highpoints of all the mountain ranges in Utah.

Details

Utah Mountain Ranges

Done Range General Location Highpoint Name Elevation (ft)
[ ] Abajo Mountains SE Utah (San Juan Co.) Abajo Peak 11,360
[ ] Antelope Range (Iron Co.) SW Utah Unnamed highpoint ~7,200
[ ] Antelope Range (Juab Co.) Central-W Utah Unnamed highpoint ~6,800
[ ] Antelope Range (Sevier Co.) Central Utah Unnamed highpoint ~7,100
[ ] Aquarius Plateau (Boulder Mtn.) South-Central Utah (Garfield Co.) Bluebell Knoll 11,328
[ ] Bear River Mountains N Utah (Cache/Box Elder Co.) Naomi Peak 9,980
[ ] Beaver Dam Mountains SW Utah (Washington Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,995
[ ] Beaver Lake Mountains SW Utah (Beaver Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,400
[ ] Black Mountains SW Utah (Iron/Beaver Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,800
[ ] Blue Spring Hills N Utah (Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,500
[ ] Buckskin Mountains SW Utah (Washington Co., AZ border) Unnamed highpoint ~6,500
[ ] Bull Valley Mountains SW Utah (Washington Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,200
[ ] Burbank Hills SW Utah (Beaver Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,800
[ ] Canyon Mountains Central Utah (Millard/Juab Co.) Fool Creek Peak 9,675
[ ] Cedar Mountains (Iron Co.) SW Utah Unnamed highpoint ~8,000
[ ] Cedar Mountains (Tooele Co.) NW Utah Unnamed highpoint ~6,980
[ ] Confusion Range W Utah (Millard Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,549
[ ] Conger Range W Utah (Millard Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,300
[ ] Crawford Mountains NE Utah (Rich Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~9,100
[ ] Cricket Mountains W Utah (Millard Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,600
[ ] Deep Creek Mountains W Utah (Tooele/Juab Co., NV border) Ibapah Peak 12,087
[ ] Drum Mountains W Utah (Millard/Tooele Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,975
[ ] Dugway Range W Utah (Tooele Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,200
[ ] East Tintic Mountains Central Utah (Utah/Juab Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,700
[ ] Escalante Mountains South-Central Utah (Garfield Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~10,600
[ ] Fish Lake Plateau South-Central Utah (Sevier/Wayne Co.) Fish Lake Hightop 11,633
[ ] Fish Springs Range W Utah (Juab Co.) George H. Hansen Peak 7,726
[ ] Gilson Mountains Central Utah (Juab Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,400
[ ] Goose Creek Mountains N Utah (Box Elder Co., ID border) Unnamed highpoint ~8,400
[ ] Grassy Mountains NW Utah (Tooele Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,000
[ ] Grouse Creek Mountains N Utah (Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~8,600
[ ] Hansel Mountains N Utah (Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,200
[ ] Harmony Mountains SW Utah (Washington Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,600
[ ] Henry Mountains SE Utah (Garfield/Wayne Co.) Mount Ellen 11,522
[ ] Hogup Mountains N Utah (Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,545
[ ] House Range W Utah (Millard Co.) Swasey Peak 9,669
[ ] Indian Peak Range SW Utah (Beaver/Iron Co.) Indian Peak 9,838
[ ] La Sal Mountains SE Utah (Grand/San Juan Co.) Mount Peale 12,726
[ ] Lake Mountains N Utah (Utah Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,000
[ ] Lakeside Mountains NW Utah (Tooele/Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,700
[ ] Mahogany Mountains W Utah (NV border) Unnamed highpoint ~6,900
[ ] Markagunt Plateau SW Utah (Iron Co.) Brian Head 11,307
[ ] Mineral Mountains SW Utah (Beaver Co.) Granite Peak 9,833
[ ] Moccasin Mountains SW Utah (Washington Co., AZ border) Unnamed highpoint ~6,400
[ ] Monte Cristo Range N Utah (Cache/Rich Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~9,148
[ ] Mountain Home Range W Utah (Beaver/Millard Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,400
[ ] Needle Mountains W Utah (NV border) Unnamed highpoint ~6,300
[ ] Newfoundland Mountains NW Utah (Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,330
[ ] Onaqui Mountains W Utah (Tooele Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~8,211
[ ] Oquirrh Mountains N Utah (Salt Lake/Tooele/Utah Co.) Flat Top Mountain 10,620
[ ] Pavant Range Central Utah (Millard/Sanpete/Sevier Co.) Mine Camp Peak 10,222
[ ] Pilot Range NW Utah (Box Elder Co., NV border) Pilot Peak 10,716
[ ] Pine Valley Mountains SW Utah (Washington Co.) Signal Peak 10,365
[ ] Promontory Mountains N Utah (Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,400
[ ] Raft River Mountains N Utah (Box Elder Co., ID border) Bull Mountain 9,934
[ ] Red Mountains NE Utah (Daggett Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~8,700
[ ] Rubber Mountains W Utah (Millard Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,200
[ ] San Francisco Mountains W Utah (Beaver/Millard Co.) Frisco Peak 9,660
[ ] San Pitch Mountains Central Utah (Sanpete Co.) Salt Benchmark 9,321
[ ] Sheeprock Mountains Central Utah (Tooele/Juab Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~9,200
[ ] Silver Island Range NW Utah (Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,590
[ ] Simpson Mountains W Utah (Juab Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,800
[ ] Stansbury Mountains N Utah (Tooele Co.) Deseret Peak 11,031
[ ] Star Range SW Utah (Beaver Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~8,200
[ ] Swasey Mountain W Utah (Millard Co.) Swasey Peak 9,669
[ ] Tavaputs Plateau (Roan Cliffs) NE Utah (Carbon/Emery/Uintah Co.) Strawberry Peak 9,469
[ ] Thomas Range W Utah (Juab Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,418
[ ] Traverse Ridge N Utah (Salt Lake/Utah Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,800
[ ] Tunnel Springs Mountains W Utah (Box Elder Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~6,700
[ ] Tushar Mountains SW Utah (Beaver/Piute/Sevier Co.) Delano Peak 12,174
[ ] Uinta Mountains NE Utah (Duchesne/Summit/Daggett Co.) Kings Peak 13,528
[ ] Valley Mountains Central Utah (Utah Co.) Unnamed highpoint ~7,000
[ ] Wah Wah Mountains W Utah (Beaver/Millard/Iron Co.) Wah Wah Mountain 9,393
[ ] Wasatch Plateau Central Utah (Carbon/Emery/Sanpete/Sevier Co.) South Tent Mountain 11,321
[ ] Wasatch Range N-Central Utah (Cache to Juab Co.) Mount Nebo 11,933
[ ] Wellsville Mountains N Utah (Box Elder/Cache Co.) Box Elder Peak 9,372
[ ] West Hills (Box Elder Co.) N Utah Unnamed highpoint ~6,900
[ ] West Hills (Juab Co.) Central-W Utah Unnamed highpoint ~6,800
Done Antelope Island Range N Utah (Great Salt Lake, Davis Co.) Frary Peak 6,596

item.title: Frary Peak
item.author: sheridad33@gmail.com (Trevor)
item.description:
Overview

Fourth hike in [Utah Range High Points](/Posts/Hiking/Utah%20Mountain%20Range%20High Points), also probably the most normal and uneventful of the bunch.

The Hike

We started from the upper lot on antelope island. The hike was very simple compared to some of our recent hikes, which felt pretty good. As we could zone out and not worry about route finding. The hike was pretty steep in spots but in general wasn’t crazy, really the perfect amount. It was a very busy hike, so Freddy had to remain on the leash. Near the top at a false summit, we mindlessly took the wrong turn and ended up traversing the rocky ridge about 300 yards to the summit. Which was much more advanced than the rest of the trail, but we realized this at the peak, and there is a perfectly easy trail that also gets there.

Fred’s Take

Fred, wasn’t thrilled to be on the leash but that paled in comparison to being outside and seeing lots of people and dogs. Each of which he wanted us to stop for. The fun train stopped though when we went off the trail at the ridge. He had to be carried over a lot of rocks, and near the top we decided to just tie him to one and give him lunch while we ran to the peak. The food only distracted him for a bit though and he was angry. Luckily he forgot pretty quick once hitting the trail. In the end he gave the hike 7/10

Stats at a Glance

Ranking Criteria

Stat Value
Miles 7
Elevation Gain 2211
Difficulty 4/10
Beauty 3/10
Solitude 1/10
Location Antelope Island

Photos

Would I Do It Again?

Yes, this is a good workout hike. Main issue is the fee to get on the island

item.title: Grassy Range High Point
item.author: sheridad33@gmail.com (Trevor)
item.description:
Overview

This was our third hike of the [Utah Range High Points](/Posts/Hiking/Utah%20Mountain%20Range%20High Points). Felt very similar to the two before (Craner Peak & Graham Peak) nestled right in the middle. From afar it looks like a smaller & less interesting range than the ones surrounding it, but we found that that is far from true.

The Hike

We started from the intersection of the dirt road that approaches the range from the east, and stopped a bit prematurely as to not have any car issues, but you could drive much closer and make this hike a lot easier. From where we started we hiked in a straight line towards the peak, which meant some harder hiking but also allowed us to explore even more. We quickly came upon a cliff side with what looked like a small cave from afar, and after getting close we found the small overhang but more impressive were the rocks. The cliff have veins of white and black rock that formed a intricate maze. Further along there was a rock that had crystals forming out of it. As we progressed we saw massive junipers and sandstone peaking out from the dirt. The straight line though was brutal, going straight up and then right on down loose rock. The final climb to the peak wasn’t easy, from the weird path we had taken it landed us on the steepest part of the face. At the top there is a jar to sign your name, and as expected it hadn’t welcomed many visitors

Fred’s Take

Fred, came in to this with a few de-buffs. On the dirt road in he threw up twice and after that we had him chase the car as to not get more sick. However even with that he was full of energy, and had a great time, until he didn’t. Along the way he found many bones from long gone animals, making sure to worry my with the chance of a random parasite. He started to fade after seeing the face of rock we had to climb after going up and down for hours. It was steep but he chugged right on up, but his resolve ran thin near the top. Requiring a small lift up the last obstacle. At the peak he gave the peak 3/10 but after a nice meal, he felt a bit better. On the way down he cheered up and by the time we hit flat ground he forgot about his prior rating. At the car he said 8/10.

Stats at a Glance

Ranking Criteria

Stat Value
Miles 7.8
Elevation Gain 2979
Difficulty 6.6/10
Beauty 5.8/10
Solitude 8/10
Location Tooele

Photos

Would I Do It Again?

I would love to explore this cliff we found and look around the mountains more, but the peak itself, probably not. Unless I did a different route up.
